Atom Computing
Atom Computing builds quantum computers using optically-trapped neutral atoms to create arrays of nuclear-spin qubits.
Our commercial platform, AC1000, harnesses the power of over 1,200 qubits to explore innovative quantum algorithms, pushing the boundaries of system performance and scale.
Atom Computing builds quantum computers using optically-trapped neutral atoms to create arrays of nuclear-spin qubits.
Our commercial platform, AC1000, harnesses the power of over 1,200 qubits to explore innovative quantum algorithms, pushing the boundaries of system performance and scale.
We employ the nuclear spin of neutral atoms to construct qubits.

Leveraging our technology advantage, AC1000 offers better-than-threshold fidelities, 1,200+ physical qubits, all-to-all qubit connectivity, and advanced technical capabilities such as mid-circuit measurement with qubit reuse and reset and real-time conditional branching.

role_in_text: Developer of the AC1000 quantum computing system
occurrence_sentence: Atom's AC1000 enables users to push the limits on quantum error correction research and explore quantum applications.

role_in_text: Developer of the AC1000 quantum computing system
occurrence_sentence: With highly competitive fidelities, 1,200+ physical qubits, all-to-all qubit connectivity, and advanced technical capabilities such as mid-circuit measurement with qubit reuse and reset and real-time conditional branching, Atom's AC1000 enables users to push the limits on quantum error correction research and explore quantum applications.

Atom Computing is developing large-scale quantum computers to enable companies and researchers to achieve unprecedented computational breakthroughs.
